summary: Replayed ADR 0295 from the latest origin/main build-artifact-cache implementation on docker-build-lv3, kept the governed ADR 0191 in-place exception explicit, refreshed the guest seed plan from 41 to 50 repo-derived warmable images, warmed the new mirror set, and re-verified BuildKit, apt-cacher-ng, and Docker bridge forward compatibility afterward.

verification.check: Repo validation and targeted artifact-cache regression coverage on the replay branch
verification.observed: `./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces data-models` passed after the replay branch was registered in `workstreams.yaml`; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-docs` intentionally exposed stale canonical truth while the workstream was marked `in_progress`; `uvx --from pyyaml python scripts/canonical_truth.py --write` refreshed `README.md` and unblocked the governed wrapper; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` passed with the existing non-blocking ADR index warning; and `uv run --with pytest python -m pytest -q tests/test_artifact_cache_seed.py tests/test_artifact_cache_runtime_role.py tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_service_id_resolver.py` returned `18 passed in 1.10s`.

verification.check: Scoped build-artifact-cache automation path on the fresh replay worktree
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/ansible_scope_runner.py run --inventory inventory/hosts.yml --run-id ws0295syntax-r2 --playbook playbooks/services/build-artifact-cache.yml --env production -- --private-key .local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump --syntax-check` passed before the live replay.

Direct SSH verification through the Proxmox jump path confirmed `PORT:5001=ok`, `PORT:5002=ok`, `PORT:5003=ok`, and `PORT:5004=ok`; all four `artifact-cache-*` containers were already up; `systemctl is-active lv3-buildkitd` returned `active`; `docker buildx inspect lv3-cache --bootstrap` succeeded; `apt-cacher-ng` served its local report page; but `jq '.seed_images | length' /opt/artifact-cache/seed-plan.json` still returned the older `41`-image warm set.

`ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=build-artifact-cache env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` succeeded from latest `origin/main` base commit `aaab0fef79957f2c8b045b791fb117244d8873ec`, recorded the expected promotion-bypass event and ADR 0191 override, and finished with `docker-build-lv3 : ok=122 changed=10 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=8 rescued=0 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Post-apply cache-plane and Docker bridge verification
verification.observed: Post-apply SSH verification confirmed all four mirror endpoints still responded; the four `artifact-cache-*` containers stayed up; `jq '.seed_images | length' /opt/artifact-cache/seed-plan.json` now returned `50`; local `scripts/artifact_cache_seed.py` against current repo catalogs also returned `50`; `systemctl is-active lv3-buildkitd` returned `active`; `docker buildx inspect lv3-cache --bootstrap` succeeded; `apt-cacher-ng` still served `http://127.0.0.1:3142/acng-report.html`; and `sudo nft list chain inet filter forward` showed accepts for both `172.16.0.0/12` and `192.168.0.0/16`.

notes: This replay confirmed that the cache plane itself remained healthy between live applies, but the guest-side seed plan had drifted behind the current repo catalogs at 41 warmable images. The March 30 replay refreshed that drift back to the latest-main 50-image set.

The replay intentionally used `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` as the documented ADR 0191 narrow exception for `docker-build-lv3`; immutable guest replacement remains the preferred steady-state delivery path until ADR 0296 moves the cache plane to a dedicated guest.

The shared live-apply wrapper also reasserted Docker bridge forward compatibility on `docker-build-lv3`, including a Docker restart when live bridge chains were missing and nftables accepts for `172.16.0.0/12` and `192.168.0.0/16` afterward.

No `VERSION`, `platform_version`, `changelog.md`, `versions/stack.yaml`, or top-level README status-summary bump was required for this replay because ADR 0295 was already represented in canonical mainline truth; this receipt refreshes latest evidence only.
